steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject is "Recordings of electrical activity in the brain," — a noun phrase followed by a comma. The blank must supply an appositive that renames that subject and a main verb to complete the sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘electrograms show that while responding to hypothetical match scenarios, the most highly skilled soccer players have’: "electrograms" correctly renames "Recordings" (an electrogram is a recording of electrical activity), and "show" serves as the main verb. The sentence reads logically. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘hypothetical match scenario responses show that the most highly skilled soccer players captured in electrograms have’: "responses" is not a synonym for "recordings" — the appositive is semantically wrong. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘the most highly skilled soccer players responding to hypothetical match scenarios have electrograms that show’: inverts the logic; players are subjects of the recording, not possessors of it. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘responses to hypothetical match scenarios show that the most highly skilled soccer players have electrograms with’: "responses to hypothetical match scenarios" is not a synonym for "Recordings of electrical activity in the brain"; the appositive is semantically wrong. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
